description: Extend Fs trait with read_dir method and DirEntry type for directory scanning
steps: Add `DirEntry` struct with `name: String` and `is_dir: bool` fields to `crates/libaipm/src/fs.rs`
Add `fn read_dir(&self, path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<Vec<DirEntry>>` to the `Fs` trait
Implement `read_dir` for the `Real` struct using `std::fs::read_dir`, collecting entries into `Vec<DirEntry>`
In the `Real::read_dir` impl, use `entry?.file_type()?.is_dir()` for the `is_dir` field and `entry?.file_name().to_string_lossy().into_owned()` for `name`
Update ALL existing mock `Fs` implementations in test code (e.g., `FailFs`, `CountingFs` in `init.rs`, and any in `workspace_init/` tests) to include a `read_dir` stub that returns `Ok(Vec::new())`
Run `cargo build --workspace` to verify compilation
Run `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` to verify no lint issues

description: Add unit tests for Fs::read_dir Real implementation
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/fs.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, add test `real_read_dir_lists_entries`: create a temp dir with files and subdirs, call `Real.read_dir()`, assert correct entries returned
Add test `real_read_dir_empty_dir`: create empty temp dir, assert `read_dir` returns empty Vec
Add test `real_read_dir_nonexistent`: call `read_dir` on nonexistent path, assert `io::Error` returned
Add test `real_read_dir_distinguishes_files_and_dirs`: create temp dir with both files and subdirs, assert `is_dir` flags are correct for each
Run `cargo test -p libaipm` to verify all pass
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Create migrate module with core types: Options, MigrateResult, MigrateAction, Artifact, ArtifactKind, ArtifactMetadata, Error
steps: Create directory `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/`

Create `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs` with all public type definitions: `ArtifactKind` enum (Skill, Command), `ArtifactMetadata` struct (name, description, hooks, model_invocation_disabled), `Artifact` struct, `Options` struct, `MigrateAction` enum (PluginCreated, MarketplaceRegistered, Renamed, Skipped, DryRunReport), `MigrateResult` struct, `Error` enum with thiserror derive
Add submodule declarations: `pub mod detector;`, `pub mod skill_detector;`, `pub mod command_detector;`, `pub mod emitter;`, `pub mod registrar;`, `pub mod dry_run;`
Create stub files for each submodule (empty or with placeholder types) so the module compiles
Add `pub mod migrate;` to `crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs`
Run `cargo build -p libaipm` to verify compilation
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Implement Detector trait and claude_detectors() factory function
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/detector.rs`, define the `Detector` trait with `fn name(&self) -> &'static str` and `fn detect(&self, source_dir: &Path, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<Vec<Artifact>, Error>`
Implement `claude_detectors()` function that returns `Vec<Box<dyn Detector>>` containing `SkillDetector` and `CommandDetector`
Ensure the trait is `pub` and usable from outside the module
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`

description: Implement SkillDetector with frontmatter parsing, script reference extraction, and recursive file collection
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/skill_detector.rs`, implement `SkillDetector` struct with `Detector` trait
Implement `detect()`: scan `source_dir.join("skills")` using `fs.read_dir()`, iterate directory entries, skip non-dirs, skip dirs without `SKILL.md`

Implement `parse_skill_frontmatter(content: &str) -> Result<ArtifactMetadata, Error>`: split on `---` delimiters, extract YAML block, parse `name`, `description`, `hooks` fields line-by-line (no YAML parser dependency â€” use simple string matching)

Implement `extract_script_references(content: &str) -> Vec<PathBuf>`: scan markdown body for `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/<path>` and `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/<path>` patterns using string matching or regex-free approach
Implement `collect_files_recursive(dir: &Path, base: &Path, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Error>`: walk directory tree using `fs.read_dir()`, return all file paths relative to `base`
Use artifact name from frontmatter `name` field if present, otherwise use directory name
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Implement CommandDetector with command-to-skill conversion logic
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/command_detector.rs`, implement `CommandDetector` struct with `Detector` trait
Implement `detect()`: scan `source_dir.join("commands")` using `fs.read_dir()`, iterate entries, skip directories, skip non-.md files
Strip `.md` extension from filename to derive the command name
Implement `parse_command_frontmatter(content: &str) -> ArtifactMetadata`: extract frontmatter if present, always set `model_invocation_disabled = true`
Reuse `extract_script_references()` from skill_detector module (consider making it a shared helper in mod.rs or a helpers submodule)
Set `artifact.files` to `vec![PathBuf::from(&entry.name)]` (single file)
Set `artifact.source_path` to the full path of the command .md file
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Implement emitter module: plugin directory creation, file copying, path rewriting, hooks extraction, manifest/JSON generation
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/emitter.rs`, implement `emit_plugin()` function per spec section 5.7
Implement `resolve_plugin_name()` for name conflict resolution: if name exists in `existing_names`, increment counter, return `{name}-renamed-{counter}`, push `MigrateAction::Renamed`
Implement `rewrite_skill_dir_paths(content, skill_name)`: replace `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/` with `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/../../scripts/`
Implement `file_is_skill_md(path)`: check if filename is `SKILL.md` (case-sensitive)
Implement command-to-skill conversion in emit: for `ArtifactKind::Command`, create `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` with frontmatter wrapper adding `disable-model-invocation: true`
Implement `convert_hooks_yaml_to_json(hooks_yaml: &str) -> Result<String, Error>`: convert YAML hooks block to JSON format for `hooks.json`
Implement `generate_plugin_manifest(artifact, plugin_name)` per spec section 5.10: produce `[package]` + `[components]` TOML
Implement `generate_plugin_json(name, metadata)` per spec section 5.11: produce JSON with name, version, description
Use `crate::workspace_init::write_file` helper (or equivalent) for writing files through Fs
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Implement registrar module: append migrated plugins to marketplace.json
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/registrar.rs`, implement `register_plugins(ai_dir, plugin_names, fs)` per spec section 5.12
Read `.ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` via `fs.read_to_string()`
Parse as `serde_json::Value`, get mutable reference to `plugins` array
For each plugin name: check if already registered (by name field), skip if so, otherwise append `{name, source, description}` entry
Serialize back with `serde_json::to_string_pretty` + trailing newline
Write back via `fs.write_file()`
Map parse errors to `Error::MarketplaceJsonParse`
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Implement dry_run module: generate structured markdown report
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/dry_run.rs`, implement `generate_report(artifacts, existing_plugins, source_name) -> String`
Generate report header: `# aipm migrate â€” Dry Run Report`, source, artifact count
Group artifacts by kind (Skills section, Legacy Commands section)
For each artifact: list source path, target path, files to copy, manifest changes, marketplace.json entry, path rewrites, hooks extraction status, conflict status
Generate summary table at the end: plugins to create, marketplace entries to add, name conflicts (auto-renamed), hooks to extract
Use the same `resolve_plugin_name` logic (or simulate it) to show what renames would occur
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Implement migrate() orchestrator function wiring detectors, emitter, and registrar
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs`, implement `pub fn migrate(opts: &Options<'_>, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<MigrateResult, Error>` per spec section 5.14
Validate prerequisites: check `.ai/` exists (return `Error::MarketplaceNotFound`), check source dir exists (return `Error::SourceNotFound`)
Resolve detectors via `detector::claude_detectors()` for `.claude` source
Run all detectors, collect artifacts into `Vec<Artifact>`
Implement `collect_existing_plugin_names(ai_dir, fs)` using `fs.read_dir()` to get existing plugin directory names as `HashSet<String>`
If `opts.dry_run`: call `dry_run::generate_report()`, write to `aipm-migrate-dryrun-report.md`, return early with `DryRunReport` action
Otherwise: iterate artifacts, call `emitter::emit_plugin()` for each, collect actions and registered names
Call `registrar::register_plugins()` to append all to marketplace.json
Return `MigrateResult` with all collected actions
Run `cargo build -p libaipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p libaipm -- -D warnings`

description: Add Commands::Migrate variant to aipm CLI and implement dispatch
steps: In `crates/aipm/src/main.rs`, add `Migrate` variant to `Commands` enum with fields: `dry_run: bool` (`#[arg(long)]`), `source: String` (`#[arg(long, default_value = ".claude")]`), `dir: PathBuf` (`#[arg(default_value = ".")]`)
Add doc comment: `/// Migrate AI tool configurations into marketplace plugins`
Add `Some(Commands::Migrate { dry_run, source, dir }) =>` match arm in `run()` function
Resolve `dir` (if `.` replace with `std::env::current_dir()?`)
Build `libaipm::migrate::Options` and call `libaipm::migrate::migrate()`
Print each `MigrateAction` to stdout using `writeln!` with `let _ =` pattern
Handle all action variants: PluginCreated, MarketplaceRegistered, Renamed, Skipped, DryRunReport
Add necessary `use` imports for `libaipm::migrate::MigrateAction`
Run `cargo build -p aipm`
Run `cargo clippy -p aipm -- -D warnings`

description: Unit tests for SkillDetector (8 tests covering all scan scenarios)
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/skill_detector.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, create a `MockFs` implementation with configurable directory listings and file contents
Add test `detect_skill_with_skill_md`: mock `.claude/skills/deploy/` with `SKILL.md`, assert returns 1 artifact with correct name, kind=Skill, and files
Add test `detect_skill_without_skill_md`: mock skill dir without SKILL.md, assert returns 0 artifacts
Add test `detect_skill_with_scripts`: mock SKILL.md body containing `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/deploy.sh`, assert `referenced_scripts` is populated

Add test `detect_skill_extracts_frontmatter`: mock SKILL.md with `---
name: my-deploy
description: Deploy app
hooks:
  PreToolUse: ...
---`, assert metadata fields populated
Add test `detect_skill_no_skills_dir`: mock `.claude/` without `skills/` subdirectory, assert returns empty Vec
Add test `detect_skill_empty_skills_dir`: mock empty `skills/` directory, assert returns empty Vec
Add test `detect_multiple_skills`: mock two skill directories, assert returns 2 artifacts
Add test `detect_skill_nested_files`: mock skill with `examples/` subdir and `reference.md`, assert all files collected recursively
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: Unit tests for CommandDetector (7 tests covering all detection scenarios)
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/command_detector.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, reuse or create a `MockFs`
Add test `detect_command_md_file`: mock `commands/review.md`, assert returns 1 artifact with kind=Command
Add test `detect_command_adds_disable_model_invocation`: assert metadata.model_invocation_disabled is true

Add test `detect_command_with_frontmatter`: mock command with `---
name: review
description: Code review
---`, assert metadata parsed
Add test `detect_command_without_frontmatter`: mock plain markdown, assert metadata has defaults + model_invocation_disabled=true
Add test `detect_command_ignores_non_md`: mock `commands/readme.txt`, assert skipped
Add test `detect_command_ignores_directories`: mock subdirectory in commands/, assert skipped
Add test `detect_no_commands_dir`: mock `.claude/` without `commands/`, assert empty Vec
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: Unit tests for emitter module (9 tests covering plugin creation, path rewriting, hooks extraction, command conversion)
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/emitter.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, create a recording `MockFs` that tracks all write operations
Add test `emit_creates_plugin_directory_structure`: assert `create_dir_all` called for plugin dir, `.claude-plugin/`, `skills/<name>/`
Add test `emit_generates_valid_aipm_toml`: capture written aipm.toml content, pass through `crate::manifest::parse_and_validate()`, assert Ok
Add test `emit_generates_plugin_json`: capture written plugin.json, parse as serde_json::Value, assert name/version/description fields
Add test `emit_copies_skill_files`: assert all artifact.files are written to correct destinations
Add test `emit_copies_referenced_scripts`: assert scripts written to `scripts/` directory
Add test `emit_rewrites_claude_skill_dir`: assert `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/` replaced with `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/../../scripts/`
Add test `emit_extracts_hooks_to_json`: mock artifact with hooks metadata, assert `hooks/hooks.json` written with valid JSON
Add test `emit_command_as_skill`: mock Command artifact, assert `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` created with `disable-model-invocation: true`
Add test `emit_command_wraps_with_frontmatter`: mock command without frontmatter, assert SKILL.md has `---` wrapper with name and disable-model-invocation
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: Unit tests for name conflict resolution (3 tests)
steps: In the emitter or mod.rs `#[cfg(test)]` module, add test `resolve_name_no_conflict`: name not in existing set, assert returns unchanged name and no Renamed action
Add test `resolve_name_conflict_renames`: name exists in set, assert returns `<name>-renamed-1` and pushes Renamed action
Add test `resolve_name_multiple_conflicts`: two different names both conflict, assert sequential IDs `-renamed-1`, `-renamed-2`
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: Unit tests for registrar module (4 tests covering marketplace.json manipulation)
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/registrar.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, create a MockFs with readable/writable marketplace.json
Add test `register_appends_to_empty_plugins_array`: mock marketplace.json with empty plugins array, register 2 plugins, assert both appear in written JSON
Add test `register_appends_alongside_existing`: mock with existing `starter-aipm-plugin` entry, register new plugin, assert both old and new entries present
Add test `register_skips_already_registered`: mock with existing `deploy` entry, register `deploy` again, assert no duplicate
Add test `register_preserves_marketplace_metadata`: assert `name`, `owner`, `metadata` top-level fields unchanged after registration
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: Unit tests for dry-run report generation (4 tests)
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/dry_run.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, add test `dry_run_report_lists_all_artifacts`: pass 2 artifacts, assert report contains sections for both
Add test `dry_run_report_shows_conflict_renames`: pass artifact whose name conflicts with existing, assert report shows renamed target
Add test `dry_run_report_shows_file_list`: pass artifact with files, assert report lists each file
Add test `dry_run_report_summary_table`: assert report ends with markdown summary table with correct counts
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: Unit tests for orchestrator function (5 tests covering error paths and full flow)
steps: In `crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` module, add test `migrate_errors_if_no_ai_dir`: mock where `.ai/` does not exist, assert `Error::MarketplaceNotFound`
Add test `migrate_errors_if_no_source_dir`: mock where `.claude/` does not exist, assert `Error::SourceNotFound`
Add test `migrate_dry_run_writes_report`: set `dry_run: true`, assert report file written and no plugin directories created
Add test `migrate_empty_source`: mock empty `.claude/skills/` and no `.claude/commands/`, assert empty MigrateResult with no errors
Add test `migrate_full_flow`: mock 1 skill + 1 command, assert PluginCreated + MarketplaceRegistered actions for both
Run `cargo test -p libaipm`

description: E2E tests for aipm migrate command (14 tests with assert_cmd + tempfile)
steps: Create `crates/aipm/tests/migrate_e2e.rs` with `#![allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]` for test code
Add helper function `aipm() -> Command` using `Command::cargo_bin("aipm")`
Add helper to set up a workspace with marketplace: run `aipm init` in a temp dir
Add helper to create a skill: write `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` with sample content
Add helper to create a command: write `.claude/commands/<name>.md` with sample content
Add test `migrate_skill_creates_plugin`: set up skill, run `aipm migrate`, verify `.ai/deploy/aipm.toml` exists with correct content
Add test `migrate_command_creates_plugin`: set up command, verify `.ai/review/skills/review/SKILL.md` has `disable-model-invocation: true`
Add test `migrate_registers_in_marketplace`: verify `marketplace.json` plugins array contains new entry
Add test `migrate_does_not_enable_plugin`: verify `.claude/settings.json` does NOT contain `deploy@local-repo-plugins` in enabledPlugins
Add test `migrate_preserves_originals`: verify `.claude/skills/deploy/SKILL.md` still exists after migration
Add test `migrate_handles_name_conflict`: pre-create `.ai/deploy/` dir, run migrate, verify `deploy-renamed-1` directory created
Add test `migrate_dry_run_creates_report`: run with `--dry-run`, verify `aipm-migrate-dryrun-report.md` exists and `.ai/deploy/` does NOT exist
Add test `migrate_dry_run_no_side_effects`: verify marketplace.json unchanged after dry-run
Add test `migrate_no_ai_dir_errors`: skip `aipm init`, run migrate, assert failure with error mentioning `aipm init`
Add test `migrate_no_source_dir_errors`: init workspace but no `.claude/` dir, assert failure
Add test `migrate_empty_skills_dir`: create empty `.claude/skills/`, assert success with no plugin dirs created
Add test `migrate_multiple_skills`: create 2 skills, assert 2 plugin dirs + 2 marketplace entries
Add test `migrate_skill_with_scripts`: create skill referencing script, assert `scripts/` dir created in plugin
Add test `migrate_help_output`: run `aipm migrate --help`, assert contains `--dry-run` and `--source`
Run `cargo test -p aipm`

description: BDD feature scenarios for aipm migrate and wire into bdd.rs filter
steps: Create `tests/features/manifest/migrate.feature` with the 7 Gherkin scenarios from spec section 8.3
In `crates/libaipm/tests/bdd.rs`, add Given step: `a skill {string} exists in {string}` â€” creates the skill directory + SKILL.md in the scenario's temp dir
Add Given step: `a command {string} exists in {string}` â€” creates the command .md file
Add Given step: `a plugin directory exists at {string}` â€” creates a pre-existing directory in .ai/
Add Given step: `a workspace initialized with marketplace` â€” runs `aipm init` in the scenario root (or sets up .ai/ + marketplace.json programmatically)
Add When step: `I run {string}` â€” extend existing `run_command` to handle `aipm migrate` and its flags

Add Then steps for: plugin directory existence, file content assertions (`contains`), marketplace.json plugin presence, settings.json non-containment, file still-exists checks, output-contains assertions, dry-run report existence, no-plugin-directory-exists
Add `"migrate.feature"` to the filename filter list in `bdd.rs` main function (around line 698-709)
Run `cargo test -p libaipm -- bdd` to verify BDD scenarios pass

description: Verify all four build gates pass with zero warnings
steps: Run `cargo build --workspace` â€” must succeed with no errors
Run `cargo test --workspace` â€” must succeed with all existing + new tests passing
Run `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` â€” must succeed with zero warnings
Run `cargo fmt --check` â€” must succeed with no formatting issues

description: Verify branch coverage gate (89%) including new migrate modules
steps: Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace`
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch`
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc`
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)'`
Verify TOTAL branch column shows >= 89%
If coverage drops below 89%: identify uncovered branches in migrate modules and add targeted tests
The dry_run and emitter modules should have high coverage since all logic operates through testable Fs trait
If any TTY-dependent code is added later, exclude it via the coverage regex pattern
